title: Objects, Packfiles, Reachability, and Integrity · Graph Forensics
summary: A responder inherits a plausible but defective object-database integrity and reachability report; preserve reachable history, isolate the first causal Git-state mismatch, and retain a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in object-database integrity and reachability report and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace blobs, trees, commits, annotated tags, parent edges, and reference roots as an immutable reachable object graph.
Explain loose objects, pack indexes, delta bases, multi-pack indexes, and why storage representation does not change object identity.
Distinguish reachable, unreachable, dangling, promised, missing, and corrupt objects using fsck and reference evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Gita Integrity Preservation evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Gita Integrity Preservation assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Case G55 is a debug in a disposable repository. the reported tree is unreachable but still present rather than corrupt or promised. The learner must predict fsck classifies connectivity and names candidate unreachable objects without pruning them, run git fsck --full --no-reflogs --unreachable, explain the candidate tree is present and reachable from the retained incident commit only, test this changed condition: remove the retained incident root in the simulator and observe the commit become unreachable, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/integrity before any repack expiration or prune operation. Competency probe: Quarantine suspect storage, preserve alternate copies and refs, verify object connectivity, and defer destructive pruning until recovery proof exists.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Gita Integrity Preservation has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Gita Integrity Preservation precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G55 is a debug in a disposable repository. the reported tree is unreachable but still present rather than corrupt or promised. The learner must predict fsck classifies connectivity and names candidate unreachable objects without pruning them, run git fsck --full --no-reflogs --unreachable, explain the candidate tree is present and reachable from the retained incident commit only, test this changed condition: remove the retained incident root in the simulator and observe the commit become unreachable, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/integrity before any repack expiration or prune operation. Competency probe: Quarantine suspect storage, preserve alternate copies and refs, verify object connectivity, and defer destructive pruning until recovery proof exists.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Running gc is an appropriate first repair for a missing-object report.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph for the debug Gita Integrity Preservation case
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology for the debug Gita Integrity Preservation case
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation for the debug Gita Integrity Preservation case
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path for the debug Gita Integrity Preservation case

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-integrity-preservation-1wwqk3" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Integrity Preservation into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-integrity-preservation-1wwqk3
# scenario-key: trace-1wwqk3 probe-3kqww1 boundary-wqk31w owner-1wwqk3x fault-1wwqk3z
# before-ref: main=a11ce1w candidate=b00b1k3
# hypothesis: the reported tree is unreachable but still present rather than corrupt or promised
git fsck --full --no-reflogs --unreachable
# expected: fsck classifies connectivity and names candidate unreachable objects without pruning them
# observation: the candidate tree is present and reachable from the retained incident commit only
# changed-case: remove the retained incident root in the simulator and observe the commit become unreachable
# after-ref: main=a11ce1w candidate=c0ffee3
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: create refs/recovery/integrity before any repack expiration or prune operation
# transfer-limit: quarantine and copy real object storage before repair because this simulator has no pack corruption
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Gita Integrity Preservation structural check is only one gate. object-database integrity and reachability report must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Quarantine suspect storage, preserve alternate copies and refs, verify object connectivity, and defer destructive pruning until recovery proof exists.
